title: 5 Settlement of AFT Returns
body_text:
5	Settlement of AFT Returns
5.1	Settlement of AFT Returns
For the settlement of AFT returns, financial institutions can have either the offsetting return entry or no offsetting return entry. If a financial institution decides to have the offsetting return entry, each return generates two transactions.
Financial institutions without the offsetting return entry
If a financial institution has opted not to have the offsetting return entry, each return generates 1 transaction. The net balanced is posted to the financial institutionâs Central 1 account. Debit or Credit Transactions are also posted to the financial institutionâs Central 1 account and are coded as AR (AFT Returns).
Financial institutions with the offsetting return entry
If a financial institution has opted for the offsetting return entry, each return generates two transactions. The total of AFT Transactions and the offsetting entry will be netted. If the net total entry is balanced, meaning 0, there is no posting. If the net total entry doesnât equal 0, either a debit or a credit entry will be posted to the financial institutionâs Central 1 account. Credits and debits are coded as AR (AFT Returns).
Settlement to the Financial Institutionâs Central 1 Account
Each return generates two transactions:
If a return is a credit, it generates a credit (I transaction) to the originating financial institution and a corresponding offsetting debit (J transaction) to the financial institution.
If a return is a debit, it generates a debit (J transaction) to the originating financial institution and a corresponding offsetting credit (I transaction) to the financial institution.
The Offsetting Transaction, as described above, appears on the following dayâs Automated System Detail Listing (CCD/UCD). It is posted to the financial institutionâs Central 1 account as a part of the CCD/UCD total. The Settlement Register (ICSR/UCSR) provides settlement details.
Settlement to the Memberâs Account
Clearing files containing the offsetting entries described above are sent to the financial institutionâs banking service provider for posting to the appropriate accounts.
END
